tasks.title: Intake AI Endpoint + intake_data Consumer
tasks.description: Two connected orphans: (1) intake.html:548 calls POST /ai/intake which does not exist — build the endpoint. It should read intake_data for the family_group_id, pass relevant fields to Claude, and return a structured care plan draft or summary. (2) intake_data is written by intake.html but never consumed anywhere — wire it as input to the AI endpoint and consider surfacing a summary in the care plan editor. Auth: require_practitioner.
tasks.plan:
## Goal
Close two connected orphans in one shot:
- Build `POST /ai/intake` so the intake.html submit flow stops returning 404.
- Wire `intake_data` as both an input (server reads + writes it) and an output (care-plan.html surfaces its content), so it is no longer orphaned.

## Boundary changes
- **New endpoint** `POST /ai/intake` (was ⚠ MISSING). Auth `require_practitioner`. Request `{family_group_id: str, intake: dict}`. Response `{sections: {situation, goals, interventions, family_role, barriers, next_steps}, intake_summary: str}`. Side effect: writes `intake_data/{family_group_id}` server-side via Admin SDK after verifying the caller owns the family_group.
- **Modified collection** `intake_data`: producer moves from `intake.html` (client) to `api/routers/ai.py` (server, Admin SDK). New consumer: `care-plan.html` reads it for the "View intake responses" collapsible.
- **Modified collection** `care_plans`: documents written by the intake flow now carry an `intake_summary` string field alongside `sections`. No rule change (existing practitioner/family/admin rule is fine — family read of intake_summary is intended and not sensitive).
- **No rule changes.** `intake_data` rule stays practitioner-only; Admin SDK bypasses rules for the server write; care-plan.html only reads intake_data when the token has practitioner or admin claim (UI-gated).
- **No new claims, no new indexes** (intake_data is doc-id-keyed).

## Implementation steps

### 1. Registry updates first (docs/registries/)
- `endpoints.md`: replace the `POST /ai/intake ⚠ MISSING` block with a served entry — served by `api/routers/ai.py`, callers `intake.html:548`, request/response shape above, status ✓. Update the summary table row.
- `collections.md` `intake_data` section: remove the ⚠ Orphaned line; producers become `api/routers/ai.py` (server, Admin SDK after ownership check); consumers become `api/routers/ai.py` (server, for downstream Claude call) and `care-plan.html` (UI collapsible, practitioner/admin only).
- `collections.md` `care_plans` schema line: add `intake_summary?: string` to the key fields.

### 2. Backend — `api/services/claude.py`
Add `draft_intake_care_plan(intake: dict) -> dict`:
- Build a prompt that flattens the intake dict into a readable section-by-section block (client_context, current_situation, medical_history, care_needs, goals).
- Instruct Claude to return ONLY a JSON object with these exact keys: `situation`, `goals`, `interventions`, `family_role`, `barriers`, `next_steps`, `intake_summary`. Each section is a string (markdown allowed). `intake_summary` is one short paragraph (3–5 sentences) synthesizing the case.
- Call `complete(prompt, model="claude-sonnet-4-6", max_tokens=2048)`.
- Parse JSON. Strip ```json fences if present. On parse failure, return `{"sections": {}, "intake_summary": <raw_text>}` so the client still has something usable.
- Do not change `draft_care_plan` (orphan, different signature, leave alone).

### 3. Backend — `api/routers/ai.py`
Add Pydantic model `IntakeRequest(BaseModel)` with `family_group_id: str` and `intake: dict` (loose typing — the intake structure is owned by the client form).

Add handler `@router.post("/intake")`:
- `user=Depends(require_practitioner)`
- `db = get_db()`; load `family_groups/{family_group_id}`; 404 if missing; 403 if `practitioner_id != user["uid"]`.
- Write `intake_data/{family_group_id}` with `set(..., merge=True)`: `{family_group_id, practitioner_id: user["uid"], intake, updated_at: serverTimestamp(), created_at: serverTimestamp() if doc didn't exist}`. Use `firestore.SERVER_TIMESTAMP`.
- Call `draft_intake_care_plan(body.intake)`; pull `intake_summary` and the six section keys out of the result.
- Return `{"sections": {situation, goals, interventions, family_role, barriers, next_steps}, "intake_summary": ...}`.

### 4. Frontend — `intake.html`
- Remove the client-side `setDoc(doc(db, 'intake_data', familyId), ...)` write at line ~575 (success path). Server now owns this write.
- Remove the duplicate `setDoc('intake_data', ...)` write in the catch-block fallback at line ~591. The server may or may not have written it depending on where the failure occurred; client should not re-write.
- Keep the `setDoc('care_plans', familyId, ...)` write; add `intake_summary: result.intake_summary || ''` to the payload alongside `sections`.
- No change to the request body shape (`{family_group_id, intake}`).

### 5. Frontend — `care-plan.html`
- In `renderPlan()`, if `planData.intake_summary` is non-empty, render a labeled card above the section list: header "From Intake — Summary" + the summary text. Read-only.
- Add an "Intake Responses" collapsible (closed by default), shown only when the current token claims include `practitioner` or `admin` (not `family`). On first expand, `getDoc(doc(db, 'intake_data', familyId))`; render each top-level intake field (`client_context`, `current_situation`, `medical_history`, `care_needs`, `goals`) as a sub-block with its labeled sub-fields. If the doc doesn't exist, show "No intake on file."
- Plain HTML/JS, no new libraries. Reuse existing `.plan-section` styling for the summary card; the collapsible can be a `<details>` element to avoid custom toggle code.

### 6. Verification (manual — no test suite in repo)
1. Run the API locally with `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY` set; open `/docs` (dev only).
2. Sign in as a practitioner with a test family group. Navigate to `intake.html?id=<groupId>`, fill all 5 steps with synthetic content, submit.
3. Confirm: network tab shows `POST /ai/intake` 200; response has 6 section keys + `intake_summary`; the success view renders.
4. Open `care-plan.html?id=<groupId>` as the same practitioner. Confirm: the "From Intake — Summary" card appears with the synthesis; "Intake Responses" collapsible shows the raw nested intake fields when expanded.
5. Confirm Firestore: `intake_data/{groupId}` exists with the submitted form; `care_plans/{groupId}` has `sections.*` populated and `intake_summary` set, `ai_generated: true`.
6. Edge: submit as a practitioner who does NOT own the group → endpoint returns 403. Submit with an unknown `family_group_id` → 404.
7. Edge: as a family member, open `care-plan.html` for a group they belong to → the summary card renders (family read of care_plans is allowed) but the "Intake Responses" collapsible is hidden.

### 7. Bump + deploy
- Bump `api/main.py` `version="0.1.1"` → `0.1.2` in the same commit.
- Push to main per project workflow; existing CI deploys the API.

## Out of scope
- No refactor of `draft_care_plan` / `POST /ai/care-plan-draft` (still orphan; addressed by a future task if ever).
- No streaming responses; the existing fixed-interval status text in intake.html stays.
- No edit/regenerate UI for the AI output beyond the existing care-plan editor.

tasks.title: Manage Family Members UI
tasks.description: The POST /family-groups/{id}/revoke endpoint exists and is tested but has no UI. Build a 'Manage Family Members' section in client.html (or a dedicated page) that lists current family members for a group (name, email, invited_at, status) and allows the practitioner to revoke access with a confirmation step. Read family member list from family_invitations collection filtered by group_id and status=accepted. After revoke, update the list in real time.
tasks.plan:
## Boundary changes

- **Collections:** none new. `family_invitations` gains a new consumer (client.html, practitioner-side read of accepted docs for a group). No new write path.
- **Endpoints:** no new endpoints. Wires up the existing orphan `POST /family-groups/{group_id}/revoke` as its first caller.
- **Claims:** none.
- **Rules:** none. Existing `/family_invitations` rule already allows the owning practitioner to read (firestore.rules:151-154). Practitioner reading family-role `users/{accepted_by_uid}` docs is NOT allowed by current rules, so the UI displays `invited_email` as the identity — no name lookup. (Denormalising `display_name` onto the invitation at accept time is a separate follow-up if email-only proves too thin.)
- **Indexes:** existing `(family_group_id ASC, invited_at DESC)` on family_invitations (firestore.indexes.json:43-50) already backs the query — no new index needed.
- **UI:** new tab on client.html (no new page). Practitioner-only — gated by existing client.html auth (client.html:681).

## Steps

**1. Registry updates (docs first)**
- `docs/registries/collections.md` — `family_invitations` section: add client.html (practitioner-side) as a consumer (read accepted list for a group via the existing index).
- `docs/registries/endpoints.md` — `POST /family-groups/{group_id}/revoke`: change Callers from "none currently" to `client.html` (manage-family-members panel); drop the orphan ⚠ flag in body and in the summary table.
- `docs/contracts/family-member-flow.md` — UI promises section: replace "Family settings / manage family members — out of scope, deferred to a later contract" with a one-line note that the practitioner-side panel lives on client.html and wires the existing revoke endpoint.

**2. Firestore rules** — no changes. Re-verify firestore.rules:146-163: owning practitioner can read `family_invitations` where `family_group_id` matches.

**3. Firestore indexes** — no changes. The `(family_group_id ASC, invited_at DESC)` composite at firestore.indexes.json:43-50 covers `where(family_group_id==X).orderBy(invited_at desc)`.

**4. Backend** — no code changes. The existing `revoke_family_member` at api/routers/family_groups.py:226 already removes the group from `family_group_ids`, revokes refresh tokens, updates the user doc, and marks any matching `family_invitations` row as `status='revoked'` with `revoked_at`. That last write is the signal the UI's onSnapshot listener uses to drop the member in real time.

**5. Frontend — client.html**
  - Add a new tab button after the existing tabs (client.html:485-489): `<button class="tab" data-tab="family">Family Members</button>`.
  - Add a new `<div class="tab-panel" id="panel-family">` after the Crisis Card panel (after client.html:635). Contains a section-card with header "Family Members" and a body div `id="familyMembersList"` plus an empty state.
  - Add an `API_BASE` constant near the top of the script section (mirroring new-client.html:640): `const API_BASE = 'https://careguide-api-658340465706.us-central1.run.app';`. (client.html currently has none.)
  - Add `subscribeFamilyMembers(familyId)` — uses Firestore `onSnapshot` on `query(collection(db,'family_invitations'), where('family_group_id','==',familyId), orderBy('invited_at','desc'))`. Renders only docs where `status === 'accepted'`. Each row shows: `invited_email`, `invited_relationship` (when present), formatted `accepted_at` (fallback `invited_at`), and a `Revoke access` button. Button is disabled with tooltip if `accepted_by_uid` is missing (defensive — older docs pre-uid-capture).
  - Add `revokeFamilyMember(uid, email)` — `confirm(\`Revoke access for ${email}? They will lose access immediately.\`)`; POST to `${API_BASE}/family-groups/${familyId}/revoke` with `{uid}` body and `Authorization: Bearer ${idToken}`. On non-2xx, `showToast(...)` a friendly error. On success, do nothing — onSnapshot picks up the status flip.
  - Wire `subscribeFamilyMembers(familyId)` into the page load flow alongside the other loaders; keep the unsubscribe handle for navigation cleanup.
  - Empty state copy: "No family members have accepted invitations yet."

**6. Wiring check**
  - Producer: `revoke_family_member` writes `status='revoked'` on the invitation (api/routers/family_groups.py:283).
  - Consumer: new onSnapshot listener filters to `status==='accepted'`, so the revoked member disappears within ~1s.
  - Practitioner-only: client.html gates to practitioner|admin (client.html:681), and `/family_invitations` rule requires `ownsGroup`, so a non-owner practitioner cannot read the list.

**7. Verification (manual, end-to-end)**
  1. Sign in as a practitioner who owns a family group with at least one accepted family member (use existing data or run the invite + accept flow).
  2. Open `/client.html?id={groupId}`; click the new **Family Members** tab.
  3. Confirm the list shows the family member's email and accepted date.
  4. Click **Revoke access**, confirm the prompt.
  5. Row disappears within ~1s.
  6. In a separate browser signed in as that family member, next request to `/family.html` fails / forces re-auth (refresh tokens were revoked) and the group is no longer accessible.
  7. Multi-group case: revoke when the user has multiple group memberships — confirm only the one group is removed (verify via response `remaining_groups` or Firebase console).
  8. Idempotent case: revoke a uid already missing the group — endpoint returns `revoked: false`; UI should not throw.
  9. Legacy case: an `accepted` invitation missing `accepted_by_uid` — Revoke button stays disabled with tooltip; console log surfaces the doc id.

## Dependencies
None — task is independent.

## Files touched (expected)
- `client.html` (new tab + panel + ~60 lines of JS + API_BASE constant)
- `docs/registries/collections.md`
- `docs/registries/endpoints.md`
- `docs/contracts/family-member-flow.md`

No backend, rules, or index changes.

tasks.title: Practitioner Messaging UI
tasks.description: family_messages collection has rules for practitioner read/write but client.html has no messaging UI. Build a messaging thread in client.html that shows the full family_messages thread for a group (ordered by created_at) and allows the practitioner to post replies. Family members already send messages from family.html. This closes the two-way messaging loop. Real-time via onSnapshot.
tasks.plan:
## Goal Close the practitioner side of family messaging. `family_messages` is currently a write-only orphan from `family.html`; this task adds a real-time message thread in `client.html` (practitioner view) and lets the practitioner reply, completing the two-way loop with no schema or rule changes. ## Boundary changes - **Modified collection** `family_messages` — adds practitioner-side producer (`client.html` addDoc reply) and practitioner-side consumer (`client.html` onSnapshot thread reader). Same schema as the existing family-side write so threads round-trip cleanly: `{family_group_id, from_uid, from_email, from_name, text, created_at: serverTimestamp(), read: false}`. - **No rule changes.** Existing `match /family_messages/{messageId}` rule already permits both practitioner-owner and family-member read/create via `canAccessFamilyData(...)` with a `from_uid == request.auth.uid` constraint on create. The practitioner is covered by `ownsGroup(groupId)` inside `canAccessFamilyData`. - **New index** `family_messages` (family_group_id ASC, created_at ASC) — required for the ordered thread query (`where family_group_id == X orderBy created_at asc`). Add to `firestore.indexes.json` and deploy. - **No new endpoints, no new claims.** Direct Firestore writes match the established pattern for `care_updates` / `session_notes` in `client.html`. - **No mark-as-read in v1.** The current rule only allows the sender (or admin) to update a message, so the recipient cannot flip `read: false → true` without a field-level rule change. Out of scope — see below. ## Implementation steps ### 1. Registry updates first (docs/registries/) - `collections.md` `family_messages` section: - Replace the existing single producer / no-consumer block with: - **Schema (key fields):** `family_group_id`, `from_uid`, `from_email`, `from_name`, `text`, `created_at`, `read` (bool, currently unused on read-receipt side — see Notes). - **Producers:** `family.html:646` (family-member side, addDoc); `client.html` postMessage (practitioner-side reply, addDoc). - **Consumers:** `client.html` Messages tab — `onSnapshot(query(family_messages, where family_group_id == X, orderBy created_at asc))`. - Drop the ⚠ orphan note; replace with a Notes line: "`read` field is written but never updated — recipient cannot toggle it under current rule. Read-receipts deferred until a field-level rule allowing recipient writes to `read` is added." - **Indexes:** add `family_messages` (family_group_id ASC, created_at ASC). - `endpoints.md`: no change — no API endpoint involved. - `claims.md`: no change. ### 2. Firestore index — `firestore.indexes.json` Append to the `indexes` array: ```json { "collectionGroup": "family_messages", "queryScope": "COLLECTION", "fields": [ { "fieldPath": "family_group_id", "order": "ASCENDING" }, { "fieldPath": "created_at", "order": "ASCENDING" } ] } ``` Deploy: `firebase deploy --only firestore:indexes` (CI may handle this — confirm before manual run). Index build is non-blocking but the query will fail until it's ready, so deploy before pushing the client change OR ship together and tolerate a brief window where the tab shows a load error (acceptable; no data risk). ### 3. Frontend — `client.html` **3a. Tab + panel markup.** - Add a new tab button between `Care Updates` and `Medications` in the `#tabs` row: ``. - Add a new `
` block right after the Care Updates panel (~line 575), modeled on the Care Updates layout: - One `section-card` titled "Family Messages" with a small subtitle: "Real-time conversation with family members on this account." - Body contains: - A scrollable thread container `#messageThread` (max-height 480px, overflow-y auto, padding 8px, light divider between messages). Empty state: `

No messages yet. Family members can send messages from their family portal.

`. - A reply composer below the thread: `

tasks.title: Profession + Beta Claims Audit
tasks.description: profession and beta claims are set during registration and approval but checked nowhere. Audit and decide: (a) wire profession claim into dashboard filtering or UI labeling (e.g. show discipline badge on practitioner profile), (b) wire beta claim into feature flagging for any beta-only features, or (c) if neither has a real use case yet, remove the claim setters to reduce orphan surface. Document the decision in claims registry regardless.
tasks.plan:
## Decision
Take path (c) for **both** claims: **remove the setters**. Rationale:
- `profession` is data, not access control. The `users/{uid}` Firestore doc already holds `profession` and `profession_label`, and every place that needs it (`family.html:631` care-team role, `admin-approvals.html:388` profession label) reads the doc. The token claim is never read, so it's a parallel source of truth that can drift if an admin edits Firestore directly.
- `beta` was reserved for future billing gating, but real billing gating will need to check Stripe subscription state or the live `users/{uid}.beta` field — not a cached token claim that goes stale until the user re-authenticates. No current code reads it. Document the path forward in the registry; do not pre-bake a flag we don't yet check.
- No production consumers means **zero blast radius** for removal.

## Boundary changes
- **Claims** `profession`, `beta` — remove from setters (`api/routers/users.py` register + status). No consumers to update.
- **No Firestore collection changes.** `users/{uid}.profession`, `profession_label`, `beta` fields all stay — they are the source of truth and are actively consumed.
- **No endpoint shape changes.** `/users/register` and `/users/{uid}/status` keep the same request/response (response of register still includes `profession` and `beta` echoes from the request body — purely informational, not from claims).
- **No rule changes.** `firestore.rules` does not reference either claim.
- **No index changes.**
- **Stale claims on existing accounts:** harmless (no reader). No migration script. They will be cleared naturally the next time `set_user_status` runs for that user (active path now writes only `{practitioner: True}`; rejected path already writes `{}`).

## Implementation steps

### 1. Registry updates first (docs/registries/claims.md)
- Replace the `profession` and `beta` claim section bodies with a short "Removed" note so future readers searching for the claim name find the rationale:
  - `profession`: "**Removed (audit 2026-05-17).** Profession is data, not access control. The single source of truth is `users/{uid}.profession` + `profession_label`, consumed by `family.html` (care-team role) and `admin-approvals.html` (label). No token claim is set."
  - `beta`: "**Removed (audit 2026-05-17).** No consumers. Future billing gating should read live state (Stripe subscription, or `users/{uid}.beta`) rather than a cached token claim that goes stale until re-auth. No token claim is set."
- Update the summary table at the bottom: remove `profession` and `beta` rows.
- Update the `practitioner` claim **Setters** lines so they no longer imply profession/beta are set in the same call.

### 2. Backend — `api/routers/users.py`
- In `register_practitioner` (~line 57), change:
  ```python
  firebase_auth.set_custom_user_claims(uid, {
      "practitioner": True,
      "profession":   body.profession,
      "beta":         body.beta,
  })
  ```
  to:
  ```python
  firebase_auth.set_custom_user_claims(uid, {"practitioner": True})
  ```
- In `set_user_status` active branch (~line 254), change:
  ```python
  firebase_auth.set_custom_user_claims(uid, {
      "practitioner": True,
      "profession":   profession,
      "beta":         user_data.get("beta", True),
  })
  ```
  to:
  ```python
  firebase_auth.set_custom_user_claims(uid, {"practitioner": True})
  ```
  The local `profession` variable (~line 249) becomes unused — remove it.
- The rejected branch already writes `{}` — leave alone.
- The response payload of `/register` still references `body.profession` and `body.beta` — leave those (they are echoes of the request body, not from claims, and the client doesn't actually use them).
- **Do not** touch the Firestore write — `profession`, `profession_label`, `beta`, etc. all remain on the user doc.
- Bump `api/main.py` `version` patch number in the same commit per project rule.

### 3. Frontend
- **No changes.** Verified: `dashboard.html` does not read profession at all; `family.html:631` reads `p.profession` from the Firestore user doc (unaffected); `admin-approvals.html:388` uses Firestore field + local `PROFESSION_LABELS` map (unaffected); `login.html` redirects only on `practitioner` / `admin` / `family` claims (unaffected). No `idTokenResult().claims.profession` or `.beta` references exist anywhere.

### 4. Verification (manual)
1. With API running locally, register a new test practitioner via `signup.html`. In Firebase Auth console (or `firebase_auth.get_user(uid).custom_claims`), confirm the user has **only** `{"practitioner": True}` — no `profession`, no `beta`.
2. As admin, open `admin-approvals.html`; approve and then reject the test user. After approval: claims = `{"practitioner": True}`. After rejection: claims = `{}`.
3. In browser dev tools on `dashboard.html` after sign-in as the test practitioner, run `await firebase.auth().currentUser.getIdTokenResult()` and inspect `result.claims` — confirm no `profession` or `beta` keys.
4. Open `admin-approvals.html` — confirm the profession label still renders correctly for both newly-registered and pre-existing users (it reads `users/{uid}.profession`, not the claim).
5. As a family member, open `family.html` — confirm the care-team card still shows the practitioner's profession (reads `users/{practitioner_id}.profession`, unaffected).
6. Spot-check Firestore: `users/{uid}` doc still has `profession`, `profession_label`, and `beta` fields populated — only the claim is gone.

### 5. Out of scope (notes for future)
- `family.html:631` renders `p.profession` as the raw key (`gcm`, `elder_law`, ...) rather than the friendly `p.profession_label`. Pre-existing inconsistency; not a claim issue. Leaving for a separate follow-up task if Scott wants to standardize.
- If billing gating is added later (Stripe wiring), implement it against live subscription state, not a re-introduced `beta` claim.

tasks.title: Env-Aware API Base URL
tasks.description: Frontend hardcodes the prod Cloud Run URL (`https://careguide-api-658340465706.us-central1.run.app`) in 9 HTML files. Stage and prod each have their own Cloud Run service now (`careguide-api` and `careguide-api-stage`) but nothing picks per-environment, so `stage.parentalcareguide.com` still calls prod. Create a single shared helper `js/api-base.js` that sets `window.API_BASE` from `window.location.hostname` — hostnames starting with `stage.` use the stage Cloud Run URL; everything else uses prod. Include the helper as a `<script>` in every page that calls the API and replace each hardcoded URL with `${API_BASE}/path`. Affected pages (per grep for the prod URL string): admin-approvals.html, accept-invite.html, contact.html, dashboard.html, intake.html, new-client.html, registries.html, signup.html, verify-email.html. Also update doc references in docs/registries/endpoints.md and docs/backlog.json plan fields where the prod URL appears literally. The stage Cloud Run service URL must be confirmed after the first stage api deploy creates the service — Cloud Run URLs follow the pattern `{service}-{project-hash}.{region}.run.app`, expected to be `https://careguide-api-stage-658340465706.us-central1.run.app` but verify. After this task, stage frontend talks to stage API and prod frontend talks to prod API, automatically.
tasks.plan: ## Goal Make every browser page pick the right Cloud Run API automatically based on the hostname it was served from, so `stage.parentalcareguide.com` calls `careguide-api-stage` and the prod domains keep calling `careguide-api`. No build step, no env injection — a single tiny IIFE that runs before any module script. ## Boundary changes - **No new collections, endpoints, claims, rules, or indexes.** This task only changes how existing endpoints are addressed from the browser. - **New shared frontend asset** `js/api-base.js` — classic (non-module) script that sets `window.API_BASE` synchronously at parse time. New producer; consumed by every HTML page that currently hardcodes the prod Cloud Run URL. - **Modified frontend files** (8 callers + 1 display-only): `admin-approvals.html`, `accept-invite.html`, `contact.html`, `dashboard.html`, `intake.html`, `new-client.html`, `signup.html`, `verify-email.html` (callers), plus `registries.html` (display text only — base URL line). Each loses its hardcoded URL and gains a `` tag in ``. - **Modified docs**: `docs/registries/endpoints.md` (Base URL header), this `docs/backlog.json` plan field. Task #2's plan also references the URL literally — leave alone (it's a historical record of a completed task; rewriting in-flight plan text is churn). ### Hostname → API mapping - `stage.parentalcareguide.com` (the only stage hostname per `.github/workflows/deploy.yml:24-25`) → `https://careguide-api-stage-658340465706.us-central1.run.app` - Everything else (prod domains `parentalcareguide.com` / `www.parentalcareguide.com`, Firebase hosting fallbacks `careguide-def76.web.app` / `.firebaseapp.com`, and `localhost` during dev) → `https://careguide-api-658340465706.us-central1.run.app` `localhost` defaulting to prod matches current behavior (current hardcoded value is prod). Out of scope to add a third localhost branch. ### Stage Cloud Run URL — verification The stage service is provisioned by `.github/workflows/deploy-api.yml:34-45` on push to `stage` when `api/**` changes. As of `git log origin/stage`, no commit has touched `api/**` on the stage branch, so the service may not exist yet. **Step 0 below** triggers the first deploy and captures the actual URL — do NOT assume the expected value without verifying. Expected URL pattern: `{service}-{project-number}-{region}.run.app` → `careguide-api-stage-658340465706.us-central1.run.app`. Prod follows the same convention (`careguide-api-658340465706.us-central1.run.app`) so the expectation is well-grounded, but Cloud Run can mint different hash suffixes in some accounts. Confirm before committing the helper. ## Implementation steps ### 0. Confirm the stage Cloud Run URL (before any code change) - Trigger the stage API deploy manually: `gh workflow run "Deploy API to Cloud Run" --ref stage` (or via GitHub UI → Actions → Deploy API to Cloud Run → Run workflow on `stage`). - After completion, capture the service URL: `gcloud run services describe careguide-api-stage --region=us-central1 --format='value(status.url)'`. - If the URL differs from `https://careguide-api-stage-658340465706.us-central1.run.app`, use the actual value in step 2. - Optional smoke check: `curl -sS /health` should return `200 OK` with the health payload. ### 1. Registry updates first (docs) - `docs/registries/endpoints.md` line 7: replace `Base URL (production): \`https://careguide-api-658340465706.us-central1.run.app\`` with a two-line block: - `Base URL (production): \`https://careguide-api-658340465706.us-central1.run.app\` (service: \`careguide-api\`)` - `Base URL (stage): \`https://careguide-api-stage-658340465706.us-central1.run.app\` (service: \`careguide-api-stage\`)` - Add one-sentence note: "Browser pages pick automatically via `js/api-base.js` based on `window.location.hostname` (`stage.` prefix → stage; else prod)." - No changes to per-endpoint sections — the base URL switch is transparent to each route. ### 2. Create `js/api-base.js` Classic IIFE so it sets the global synchronously at parse time (modules are deferred — a module script in the same `` can safely read `window.API_BASE` because the classic script ran first): ```js (function () { var host = (window.location && window.location.hostname) || ''; var STAGE_API = 'https://careguide-api-stage-658340465706.us-central1.run.app'; var PROD_API = 'https://careguide-api-658340465706.us-central1.run.app'; window.API_BASE = host.indexOf('stage.') === 0 ? STAGE_API : PROD_API; })(); ``` Notes: - No `type="module"`, no `defer` — must execute before the page's module script tag is evaluated. - `host.indexOf('stage.') === 0` matches any subdomain starting with `stage.` (today only `stage.parentalcareguide.com`, but future-proof). - Update both URL constants if step 0 surfaces different values. ### 3. Wire the helper into every affected page For each file below, two edits: (a) Add `` in ``, placed AFTER ``/`<link>` tags and BEFORE the first `<script type="module">` block. The relative path works on every page (all live at the repo root; the FTP deploy uploads `js/` alongside). (b) Replace the in-page hardcoded URL constant with a read from the global. Use `const API_BASE = window.API_BASE;` everywhere for consistency, even on pages that currently name it `API`. Rename the variable at usage sites only on pages where the existing name was `API`; this minimizes churn while standardizing on `API_BASE` going forward. File-by-file: - **`accept-invite.html:211`** — replace `const API = '...';` with `const API_BASE = window.API_BASE;`. Find all uses of `API` in this file's script (likely `${API}/...`) and rename to `API_BASE`. - **`admin-approvals.html:285`** — same pattern (`API` → `API_BASE`). - **`contact.html:378`** — same pattern. - **`verify-email.html:174`** — same pattern. - **`intake.html:435`** — already `API_BASE`; replace value with `window.API_BASE`. - **`new-client.html:640`** — already `API_BASE`; replace value with `window.API_BASE`. - **`signup.html:697`** — already `API_BASE`; replace value with `window.API_BASE`. - **`dashboard.html:630`** — inline `fetch('https://careguide-api-658340465706.us-central1.run.app/auth/send-verification', ...)`. Two options: (i) declare `const API_BASE = window.API_BASE;` near the top of the module script and use `\`${API_BASE}/auth/send-verification\``, or (ii) inline `${window.API_BASE}/auth/send-verification` at the call site. Prefer (i) for consistency with the other pages, even though there's only one call site today. - **`registries.html:1154`** — display-only. The base URL appears in user-facing copy inside the Endpoints panel. Replace the static line with a dynamic two-line render so the registries page documents both envs and highlights the active one: ```html <div style="font-size:12px;color:var(--text-dim);"> Base URL: <code id="api-base-display" style="font-size:12px;color:var(--text-muted);"></code> <span id="api-env-display" style="margin-left:8px;font-size:11px;opacity:0.7;"></span> </div> ``` Add a small inline script (after `js/api-base.js` is loaded) that fills these: ```html <script> (function () { var host = window.location.hostname || ''; var env = host.indexOf('stage.') === 0 ? 'stage' : 'prod'; var el = document.getElementById('api-base-display'); var envEl = document.getElementById('api-env-display'); if (el) el.textContent = window.API_BASE; if (envEl) envEl.textContent = '[' + env + ']'; })(); </script> ``` Place the inline script at the end of `<body>` (after the panel HTML exists in the DOM). ### 4. Sanity-check that no other file slips through After the edits, run: ```bash git grep -n 'careguide-api-658340465706' ``` The ONLY remaining matches should be: - `js/api-base.js` (the `PROD_API` constant itself) - `docs/registries/endpoints.md` (the documented base URL) - `docs/backlog.json` (this plan field + task #2's historical plan) - `.github/workflows/deploy-api.yml` (CORS config — already correct) No HTML matches outside `js/api-base.js`. If any HTML file still references the prod URL, fix it before committing. ### 5. Verification (manual — no unit test suite for browser code) 1. **Prod path (local dev)**: open any affected page from `localhost` (or `127.0.0.1`). DevTools console: `window.API_BASE` should print the prod URL. Smoke-test one fetch flow per page family (e.g., `signup.html` register; `contact.html` submit; `dashboard.html` resend-verification; `accept-invite.html` accept). Each network request should target `careguide-api-658340465706.us-central1.run.app`. 2. **Prod path (deployed)**: after merge to main, open `parentalcareguide.com/signup.html` (and at least one other page). Confirm console shows prod URL and DevTools Network tab confirms requests hit the prod Cloud Run service. 3. **Stage path (deployed)**: after merge to stage, open `stage.parentalcareguide.com/signup.html`. Confirm console shows the stage URL and Network tab confirms requests hit `careguide-api-stage`. Verify CORS is happy (the stage Cloud Run service includes `https://stage.parentalcareguide.com` in `CORS_ORIGINS` per `deploy-api.yml:39`). 4. **Regression spot-checks** per page after stage promotion: - `signup.html` → register a fresh test practitioner; `/users/register` succeeds (200). - `verify-email.html?token=...` → token verifies (200) end-to-end. - `accept-invite.html` → completes invitation accept. - `contact.html` → submission succeeds. - `admin-approvals.html` → approve/reject works for an admin. - `dashboard.html` → "Resend verification email" button works for an unverified user. - `new-client.html` → invite family member round-trip works. - `intake.html` → submit the intake flow; `POST /ai/intake` succeeds (task #1 must be in stage for this). - `registries.html` → the Endpoints panel header shows the correct env URL and `[stage]` / `[prod]` tag. 5. **Firebase Hosting fallback** (`careguide-def76.web.app`): not a primary path, but if accessed it should fall through to prod (the `stage.` check is false). CORS on prod Cloud Run already lists this origin. ### 6. Out of scope - No `localhost` branch for a local API server — defer until someone actually runs the API locally and wants per-env routing. - No build/bundling step; keeping the helper as a runtime IIFE is intentional. - Task #2's plan text and the `endpoints.md` per-endpoint examples are not rewritten — only the top-level Base URL line changes. Future endpoint additions should reference `${API_BASE}` in code, not the literal URL. - No API-side bump: this task touches no `api/**` files, so `api/main.py` version is unchanged. (Step 0 manually triggers a stage API deploy of the existing HEAD without code changes — that's intentional and harmless.)

tasks.title: Orphan API Router Cleanup
tasks.description: parents, medications, and session-notes routers exist in the API but all client pages use Firestore directly instead of calling them. Decide and execute: either (a) remove the routers (simpler, reduces surface) or (b) migrate the client pages to use the API routers (adds auth enforcement at API layer, enables server-side validation). Document the decision. If removing, update endpoints registry. If keeping, wire the clients and add integration tests.
tasks.plan:
## Decision: MIGRATE (option b), not delete.

The routers already exist, are correct, and enforce ownership via `require_practitioner` + `_assert_owns_group`. Two forces pull toward keeping them:

1. **Supabase sync trajectory.** Task #8 plans to bolt a fire-and-forget Supabase upsert onto each API write endpoint. If clients keep writing to Firestore directly, those writes are invisible to the sync layer — task #8 would have to be rewritten to use Firestore triggers/Eventarc.
2. **HIPAA audit logging.** Architecture.md commits to "audit logging (key PHI access events → Cloud Logging)". A server-mediated write path is where that logging hangs cleanly; client-side writes bypass it.

Server-side validation (required fields, status enum) is a smaller secondary benefit. Reads stay on direct Firestore — they're already covered by rules, and there is no read-path benefit to a round-trip through FastAPI.

## Scope

**In scope (writes that map to existing routers):**
- `new-client.html:773` — addDoc parents → `POST /parents/`
- `client.html:897` — updateDoc parents → `PATCH /parents/{parent_id}`
- `medications.html:446` — updateDoc med edit → `PATCH /medications/{med_id}`
- `medications.html:451` — addDoc med create → `POST /medications/`
- `medications.html:464` — updateDoc med status toggle → `PATCH /medications/{med_id}` with `{status}`
- `medications.html:472` — deleteDoc med → `DELETE /medications/{med_id}`
- `client.html:1043` — addDoc session_note → `POST /session-notes/`

**Out of scope (reads):** `client.html:728-730` Promise.all and `medications.html:354` list — stay on direct Firestore. The router GETs become tagged "deferred consumer (reads on direct Firestore by design)" in the registry.

**Out of scope (writes that have no orphan-router counterpart):** `care_updates`, `family_messages`, `family_groups`, `care_plans`, `crisis_info`, `intake_data`. Those collections either have no API equivalent or are handled by other tasks (#1, #3, #7).

## Boundary changes

- **Endpoints:** no new endpoints. Three existing orphan routers (`parents`, `medications`, `session_notes`) gain their first callers:
  - `POST /parents/` ← new-client.html
  - `PATCH /parents/{id}` ← client.html parent-edit
  - `POST /medications/`, `PATCH /medications/{id}` (×2: edit + status toggle), `DELETE /medications/{id}` ← medications.html
  - `POST /session-notes/` ← client.html note-save modal
  - The `GET` endpoints in all three routers stay orphan; tag them "deferred — reads use direct Firestore by design."
- **Collections:** none new; `parents`, `medications`, `session_notes` producer lists in `collections.md` shift from HTML files to API routers.
- **Claims:** none.
- **Rules:** none. Rules already permit the practitioner; Admin SDK bypasses them server-side, so the migration is invisible at the rule layer. (Future hardening — disallowing direct client writes — is a separate task.)
- **Indexes:** none new. Existing `medications (family_group_id ASC, created_at DESC)` and `session_notes (family_group_id ASC, created_at DESC)` continue to back the read queries.

## Dependencies / sequencing

- **Task #5 (Env-Aware API Base URL) should land first.** It introduces the shared `window.API_BASE` helper that the three pages here will need. If #5 hasn't merged when #6 starts, this task should pull in the same helper (or hardcode the URL as new-client.html does today and let #5 sweep it later) — flag clearly in the PR.
- No backlog dependency on tasks #1–#3 (in stage); independent surface area.
- Task #8 (Firestore → Supabase Sync) depends on this; that task's spec assumes API-layer hooks exist.

## Implementation steps

### 1. Registry updates first (docs/registries/)

`endpoints.md`:
- `POST /parents/` — Callers: `new-client.html`. Status: ✓. Drop ⚠ orphan.
- `PATCH /parents/{parent_id}` — Callers: `client.html` (toggleEditParent save). Status: ✓.
- `POST /medications/` — Callers: `medications.html`. Status: ✓.
- `PATCH /medications/{med_id}` — Callers: `medications.html` (edit + status toggle). Status: ✓.
- `DELETE /medications/{med_id}` — Callers: `medications.html`. Status: ✓.
- `POST /session-notes/` — Callers: `client.html` (saveNote modal). Status: ✓.
- `GET` endpoints (`/parents/`, `/parents/{id}`, `/medications/`, `/medications/{id}`, `/session-notes/`, `/session-notes/{id}`) — keep `⚠ orphan` but change the reason note to: "deferred — client reads use direct Firestore by design; rules cover access. Reachable via the API for tooling or future server-side consumers."
- Update the Summary table rows accordingly.

`collections.md`:
- `parents` Producers: replace `new-client.html:773` + `client.html:865` with `api/routers/parents.py` (server, Admin SDK after ownership check).
- `medications` Producers: replace `medications.html:451` with `api/routers/medications.py`.
- `session_notes` Producers: replace `client.html:960` with `api/routers/session_notes.py`.
- Consumers lists stay the same (reads still client-side direct).
- API line at the bottom of each section: change "Orphan (no client caller — client uses direct Firestore)" to "All writes flow through this router; reads remain on direct Firestore (rule-gated)."

`claims.md`: no change.

### 2. Backend

No code changes. Routers are already correct. Double-check no schema drift by comparing each Pydantic model against the current Firestore writes from the client:
- `ParentCreate` has `care_level: str = ""` — new-client.html writes `care_level` from a select; confirm it's always sent. If new-client.html ever omits it, that's fine — Pydantic default kicks in.
- `MedicationCreate` uses `dosage` — medications.html writes `dosage` (line 446 area). Match. (Note: the field is `dose` in the registry's schema bullet — that's a registry typo to fix in step 1.)
- `SessionNoteCreate.text` — client.html saveNote sends `text` (confirmed at client.html:1043). Match. The router still writes `text` to Firestore, but the registry currently lists `body` as the field. Reconcile: the live writes use `text`, so update collections.md `session_notes` schema to `text` (not `body`).

### 3. Frontend

Pattern for every migrated write: get the Firebase ID token, POST/PATCH/DELETE to the API, handle non-2xx with the existing `showToast` (or page-specific equivalent).

Helper to add once per touched page (or factor into a tiny shared `js/api-fetch.js` — optional, see step 6):

```js
async function apiFetch(path, opts = {}) {
  const token = await auth.currentUser.getIdToken();
  const res = await fetch(\`${window.API_BASE || API_BASE}${path}\`, {
    ...opts,
    headers: {
      'Content-Type': 'application/json',
      'Authorization': \`Bearer ${token}\`,
      ...(opts.headers || {}),
    },
  });
  if (!res.ok) {
    const detail = await res.json().catch(() => ({ detail: res.statusText }));
    throw new Error(detail.detail || \`HTTP ${res.status}\`);
  }
  return res.json();
}
```

**3a. new-client.html**
- Line 624: drop `addDoc` from the firestore import if no longer used (it's still used at 748 for family_groups — leave it).
- Line 773: replace `addDoc(collection(db, 'parents'), parentData)` with `await apiFetch('/parents/', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ family_group_id: familyId, first_name: ..., last_name: ..., care_level: ..., dob, state, invite_email }) })`. Use the response `{id, ...}` if needed; current code doesn't capture the id.
- Auth: `auth.currentUser` already exists at this point in the flow.

**3b. client.html**
- Line 897 (parent update): replace `updateDoc(doc(db,'parents',parentData.id), updates)` with `await apiFetch(\`/parents/${parentData.id}\`, { method: 'PATCH', body: JSON.stringify(updates) })`.
- Line 1043 (saveNote): replace `addDoc(collection(db,'session_notes'), {...})` with `await apiFetch('/session-notes/', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify({ family_group_id: familyId, type, text }) })`. Drop `practitioner_id` and `created_at` from the request body — the router sets both server-side.
- Optimistic UI update stays as-is.

**3c. medications.html**
- Line 446 (edit save): replace `updateDoc(doc(db,'medications',editingId), data)` with `await apiFetch(\`/medications/${editingId}\`, { method: 'PATCH', body: JSON.stringify(data) })`. Strip `family_group_id` and `updated_at` from the body — neither is patch-able and the server sets `updated_at`.
- Line 451 (create): replace `addDoc(collection(db,'medications'), data)` with `await apiFetch('/medications/', { method: 'POST', body: JSON.stringify(data) })` (ensure `family_group_id` is in `data`).
- Line 464 (status toggle): replace `updateDoc(doc(db,'medications',id), { status: newStatus, updated_at: serverTimestamp() })` with `await apiFetch(\`/medications/${id}\`, { method: 'PATCH', body: JSON.stringify({ status: newStatus }) })`.
- Line 472 (delete): replace `deleteDoc(doc(db,'medications',id))` with `await apiFetch(\`/medications/${id}\`, { method: 'DELETE' })`.
- Remove now-unused imports from the firestore import statement (`updateDoc`, `deleteDoc`, `serverTimestamp`) if no other call site uses them — confirm before removing.

**3d. API_BASE handling**
- If task #5 has merged: rely on `window.API_BASE` (loaded via the `<script src="/js/api-base.js">` tag added by #5). Add the script tag to client.html and medications.html (new-client.html already has it after #5).
- If task #5 has NOT merged: hardcode `const API_BASE = 'https://careguide-api-658340465706.us-central1.run.app';` at the top of each page (mirrors the existing new-client.html pattern at line 640) and flag in the PR that task #5 should sweep these up.

### 4. Wiring check (post-edit)
- Run `grep` for residual `addDoc.*parents`, `addDoc.*medications`, `addDoc.*session_notes`, `updateDoc.*parents`, `updateDoc.*medications`, `deleteDoc.*medications` across the codebase. Should match nothing.
- Confirm each migrated call path uses `apiFetch` (or equivalent) with the correct verb and the same field names the Pydantic models expect.

### 5. Verification (manual — no test suite in repo)
1. Sign in as a practitioner. Create a new client via `new-client.html` — verify network panel shows `POST /parents/` 200 and Firestore has the parent doc with `practitioner_id` set by the server.
2. Open the new client in `client.html`. Edit the parent (toggle edit, change a field, save) — verify `PATCH /parents/{id}` 200 and Firestore reflects the change.
3. Add a session note from the modal — verify `POST /session-notes/` 200 and Firestore has `practitioner_id` + `created_at` set by the server.
4. Open `medications.html` for the client. Add a med — verify `POST /medications/` 200. Edit it — `PATCH /medications/{id}` 200. Toggle status — `PATCH /medications/{id}` with body `{status: "inactive"}`. Delete it — `DELETE /medications/{id}` 200.
5. Edge — auth failure: open the page with an expired token (or simulate by signing out a second tab and waiting); writes should surface a friendly error via `showToast`.
6. Edge — wrong-owner: have a second practitioner attempt to PATCH a parent they don't own (curl with their token). Expect 403.
7. Confirm reads still work (lists render) — they should be unchanged.

### 6. Optional cleanup (do only if trivial)
- Extract `apiFetch` into `js/api-fetch.js` and `<script>`-include it on the three touched pages, the same way #5 introduces `js/api-base.js`. This is nice-to-have; if it adds friction, leave the helper inline in each file and tag a follow-up.

### 7. Bump + deploy
- No `api/main.py` version bump — no backend code changed.
- Push to main per project workflow.

## Out of scope (defer)
- **Read migration.** Out of scope per Decision above. Reads stay on direct Firestore; `GET` endpoints remain documented as deferred consumers.
- **Tightening Firestore rules** to disallow direct client writes on `parents`/`medications`/`session_notes` (would prevent regression but risks breaking the still-allowed reads — same rule block). Track as a follow-up if/when the test suite exists to catch fallout.
- **Integration tests** for the router endpoints. Architecture targets WCAG audits and manual QA in Phase 1; the codebase has no pytest harness yet. Adding one for three routers is its own task — flag it as a deferred follow-up ("Backend Integration Test Harness") rather than smuggling in scope here.
- **Supabase write hooks** (task #8 territory). This task only sets up the call path; the upsert calls land later.

tasks.title: Supabase Project Setup + Schema
tasks.description: Initialize the Postgres/Supabase reporting layer. Tasks: (1) Create Supabase project and store credentials in env/secrets, (2) Design the reporting schema — tables for practitioners, family_groups, parents, medications, session_notes, care_updates, family_members, care_plans — with proper foreign keys and timestamps, (3) Write and run the initial migration, (4) Add Supabase Python client to api/requirements.txt, (5) Create a supabase.py service in api/services/. No data sync yet — just the foundation.

tasks.title: Firestore → Supabase Sync Layer
tasks.description: Mirror Firestore writes into Supabase for reporting. For each write endpoint in the API (register, accept-invite, family group creation, care plan updates, medications, session notes, care updates), add a fire-and-forget Supabase upsert after the Firestore write succeeds. Do not block the response on the Supabase write — log failures. Initially sync: users, family_groups, parents, medications, session_notes, care_updates, care_plans. Add a /admin/sync-status endpoint that shows last sync timestamps per collection.

tasks.title: Practitioner Analytics Dashboard
tasks.description: Build a practitioner-facing analytics section showing: active client count, session note frequency (notes per week/month), care plan completion rate, family engagement (care updates posted, family logins), medication change history. Data sourced from Supabase. New API endpoints: GET /analytics/practitioner (requires practitioner claim). New UI section in dashboard.html or a dedicated analytics.html page. Charts via a lightweight charting library (e.g. Chart.js via CDN).

tasks.title: Admin Platform Analytics
tasks.description: Build admin-facing platform analytics: total practitioners (active/pending/rejected), total family groups, total family members, care updates posted per week, new signups over time, practitioner activity (last active). Data sourced from Supabase. New API endpoints: GET /analytics/platform (requires admin claim). New section in admin-approvals.html or dedicated admin-analytics.html. Timeframe filter (7d, 30d, 90d, all-time).

tasks.title: Unblock PR queue — drop required `deploy` status check from main ruleset
tasks.description: Branch protection on main (ruleset 16509020) required a status check named `deploy` (integration_id 15368 = GitHub Actions). The job that produces that check name lives in `.github/workflows/deploy.yml` and only ran on push to main/stage, not on pull_request. Result: every PR to main sat in mergeStateStatus=BLOCKED forever, waiting for a check that by design never reported on PR head commits. Caught during /review-pr of task #2 when PRs #1, #8, and #9 all showed BLOCKED despite being MERGEABLE. Fix applied via `gh api -X PUT repos/AesopScott/careguide/rulesets/16509020` — dropped the `required_status_checks` rule, kept `deletion`, `pull_request`, `non_fast_forward`. Also opened PR #10 as an expedient attempt to make `deploy.yml` run on PRs in dry-run mode; that approach is the wrong architecture (deploy workflow shouldn't moonlight as a PR validator) and is being closed in favor of task #16. The lasting fix from this task is the ruleset change only. Emergency-fix executed mid-session from a /review-pr task #2 session.

tasks.title: Add `ci.yml` for PR-time validation; keep `deploy.yml` push-only
tasks.description: Follow-up to task #15. The right place for PR-time validation is a dedicated CI workflow, not the deploy workflow. Build `.github/workflows/ci.yml` with a `validate` job that catches the failure modes that actually matter on a PR — JSON parse errors in `docs/backlog.json`, `firebase.json`, `.firebaserc`; HTML basic syntax errors; JS `node --check` on `js/*.js`; and a guard `grep` for hardcoded prod Cloud Run URLs in HTML (the exact regression that snuck into PR #1 and was only caught by manual review). Close PR #10 without merging — its `deploy.yml` change should never land; main keeps the original push-only `deploy.yml`. After ci.yml is live, optionally re-add the required-status-check rule to ruleset 16509020 pointing at the new `validate` job name. Emergency-fix executed mid-session from a /review-pr task #2 session.
tasks.plan:
## Goal
Add a dedicated GitHub Actions workflow that catches the failure modes that actually matter on a PR (broken JSON, JS syntax errors, regressions like hardcoded prod URLs leaking into HTML), without coupling that validation to the deploy workflow. Restore `deploy.yml` to push-only by closing PR #10. Optionally re-add a required-status-check pointing at the new `validate` job once it has run green on at least one merged PR.

## Boundary changes
- **No new collections, endpoints, claims, rules, or indexes.** Pure infrastructure.
- **No registry edits required.**
- **PR #10 closed without merging.** Its `deploy.yml` change never lands; `main` keeps the original push-only `deploy.yml`.

## Files added / changed
- **New `.github/workflows/ci.yml`** — runs on `pull_request: [main, stage]` and `push: [main, stage]`. Single job `validate` with the four steps below.
- **New `scripts/validate-backlog.mjs`** — Node ESM script that schema-checks `docs/backlog.json` (required fields, unique numbers, valid status values, no orphan dep references). Kept as a separate file so it's runnable locally (`node scripts/validate-backlog.mjs`) and not buried in YAML heredocs.
- **No change to `.github/workflows/deploy.yml`.** Stays push-only.

## Implementation steps

### 1. Create `scripts/validate-backlog.mjs`
```js
#!/usr/bin/env node
import { readFileSync } from 'node:fs';

const path = 'docs/backlog.json';
const data = JSON.parse(readFileSync(path, 'utf8'));
const tasks = data.tasks ?? [];

const required = ['number', 'title', 'category', 'priority', 'status', 'dependencies', 'description'];
const validStatus = new Set(['backlog', 'ready', 'in-review', 'complete']);

const errors = [];
const seen = new Set();

for (const t of tasks) {
  for (const k of required) {
    if (!(k in t)) errors.push(`task ${JSON.stringify(t).slice(0,80)}... missing field "${k}"`);
  }
  if (seen.has(t.number)) errors.push(`duplicate task number ${t.number}`);
  seen.add(t.number);
  if (t.status && !validStatus.has(t.status)) errors.push(`task #${t.number} has invalid status "${t.status}" (expected one of: ${[...validStatus].join(', ')})`);
  if (!Array.isArray(t.dependencies)) errors.push(`task #${t.number} dependencies must be an array`);
}

// Second pass: dep references must point at existing tasks.
for (const t of tasks) {
  for (const dep of t.dependencies ?? []) {
    if (!seen.has(dep)) errors.push(`task #${t.number} references missing dep #${dep}`);
  }
}

if (errors.length) {
  for (const e of errors) console.error(`::error::${e}`);
  process.exit(1);
}

console.log(`Backlog OK: ${tasks.length} tasks validated.`);
```
- ESM (`.mjs`) so it works on `node` 18+ without `package.json` changes.
- Uses `::error::` annotations so failures surface in the GitHub PR check UI.

### 2. Create `.github/workflows/ci.yml`
```yaml
name: CI

on:
  pull_request:
    branches: [main, stage]
  push:
    branches: [main, stage]
  workflow_dispatch:

concurrency:
  group: ci-${{ github.event.pull_request.number || github.ref_name }}
  cancel-in-progress: true

jobs:
  validate:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - uses: actions/checkout@v4

      - name: Parse all JSON files
        run: |
          set -e
          for f in docs/backlog.json firebase.json .firebaserc firestore.indexes.json; do
            if [ -f "$f" ]; then
              node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('$f','utf8'))" \
                || { echo "::error file=$f::Invalid JSON"; exit 1; }
              echo "OK $f"
            fi
          done

      - name: JS syntax check (js/)
        run: |
          set -e
          shopt -s nullglob
          for f in js/*.js; do
            node --check "$f" || { echo "::error file=$f::Syntax error"; exit 1; }
            echo "OK $f"
          done

      - name: No hardcoded prod Cloud Run URL in HTML
        run: |
          if git grep -lE 'careguide-api-658340465706' -- '*.html'; then
            echo "::error::Hardcoded prod Cloud Run URL found in HTML above. Use window.API_BASE (via js/api-base.js) instead."
            exit 1
          fi
          echo "OK: no hardcoded prod URLs in HTML"

      - name: Backlog schema check
        run: node scripts/validate-backlog.mjs
```
- Concurrency cancels in-progress runs for the same PR (cheap, since this workflow is fast).
- Each step echoes "OK" on success so the run log is readable.

### 3. Close PR #10
After PR for task #16 is opened, add `Closes #10` to the PR body (so merging this also closes the deadlock-fix PR). Belt-and-suspenders: also run `gh pr close 10 --comment "Superseded by task #16 PR ({task-pr-url}) — proper CI workflow lives in ci.yml; deploy.yml stays push-only."` after this PR is opened.

### 4. Test the new check against the PR itself
- The new `.github/workflows/ci.yml` triggers on the PR that adds it. Confirm in the PR's Checks tab that the `validate` job runs and reports green.
- Manual sanity sweep before merge:
  1. Temporarily break JSON (add a trailing comma to `docs/backlog.json`), push, confirm the `Parse all JSON files` step fails red.
  2. Revert.
  3. Temporarily add a hardcoded prod URL to an HTML file (e.g., `<!-- careguide-api-658340465706.us-central1.run.app -->`), push, confirm `No hardcoded prod Cloud Run URL in HTML` fails red.
  4. Revert.
  - Both reverts pushed before requesting merge.

### 5. Verification (after merge)
- Open any noop PR (e.g., a comment change) and confirm the `validate` check runs and reports green.
- The deploy workflow does NOT run on that PR (since PR #10's change never landed — the original push-only trigger stays in place).

## Out of scope (notes for future tasks)
- **HTML linting.** Considered (`htmlhint`, `html-validate`); hand-rolled HTML in this repo generates too many false positives for v1. Add when there is a real quality issue to catch.
- **Firestore rules linting.** Requires the firebase CLI + credentials in CI. Worth a separate task if rule typos start slipping through.
- **CSS / a11y checks.** WCAG 2.1 AA is a Phase 1 deliverable per `Architecture.md`; an automated check (axe-core CLI) is a candidate for a follow-up CI step.
- **Re-adding the required-status-check rule.** Once `validate` has run green on at least one merged PR, optionally PUT the ruleset 16509020 back with `required_status_checks: [{context: "validate", integration_id: 15368}]`. Out of scope for this task — leave un-required until the check is proven reliable.
- **Symmetric API CI.** `deploy-api.yml` only deploys when `api/**` changes, and the API has no PR-time validation today either. A `python -m py_compile api/**/*.py` check (or pytest if a harness lands) is a candidate follow-up.

## Verification (manual — no test suite in repo)
Covered in Step 4 above.

## Bump + deploy
- No `api/main.py` change — no version bump.
- Frontend deploy unchanged.
